21 January 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • U.S. Forces Launch Mission in Syria to Transfer ISIS Detainees to Iraq CENTCOM 21 Jan 2026 -- U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) launched a new mission to transfer ISIS detainees from northeastern Syria to Iraq, Jan. 21, to help ensure the terrorists remain in secure detention facilities.
  • US forces transferring Daesh prisoners from Syria to Iraq: CENTCOM Press TV 21 Jan 2026 -- The US military's Central Command (CENTCOM) has begun transferring Daesh prisoners from northeastern Syria to "secure" detention facilities in Iraq, allegedly to thwart the threat they pose to the region and the US.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • In Davos, President Trump Outlines Bold Vision for American Prosperity, Transatlantic Strength The White House 21 Jan 2026

  • Trump Rules Out Force In Greenland, Walks Back Tariffs Plan RFE/RL 21 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has ruled out military action to take control of Greenland while also saying he wants to hold more talks on the United States acquiring the island, which he described as "our territory" during a keynote speech at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, on January 21.
  • Trump claims not to use force to acquire Greenland, yet WEF speeches expose widening US-Europe divide Global Times 21 Jan 2026 -- The US and its traditional allies in Europe and Canada appeared increasingly divided in Davos, Switzerland, as a string of Western leaders voiced direct or thinly veiled concerns over Washington's recent policies during the ongoing World Economic Forum's annual meeting, while US President Donald Trump also turned his criticism toward Europe.
  • California governor tells European leaders to "have a backbone" over Greenland People's Daily 21 Jan 2026 -- California Governor Gavin Newsom on Tuesday criticized European political leaders for what he described as their "complicity" in failing to stand up to U.S. President Donald Trump's demands regarding Greenland.
  • Trump Says He Respects Greenland, Denmark, But Only US Can Defend Island Sputnik 21 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that he respects Greenland and Denmark tremendously, but only the United States can defend the Arctic island.
  • Trump Warns EU's 'Trade Bazooka' to Ricochet, Vows Response From US Sputnik 21 Jan 2026 -- Anything Europe does against the United States will ricochet back, US President Donald Trump said.
  • Defence Secretary Denmark Press Conference UK MOD 21 Jan 2026 -- Defence Secretary Denmark press conference opening remarks on Defence and security cooperation.
  • Canadian PM voices support for Greenland, Denmark, opposing U.S. tariffs in Davos People's Daily 21 Jan 2026 -- Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Tuesday expressed strong support for Greenland and Denmark and opposition to tariffs proposed by the United States.
  • Trump's post of image suggesting U.S. control over Greenland disrespectful: Greenlandic PM People's Daily 21 Jan 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump's post of an image suggesting U.S. control over Greenland is disrespectful, said Greenlandic Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen on Tuesday.
  • Ukraine Reconstruction Plan Derailed at Davos Amid US-EU Feud Over Greenland - Reports Sputnik 21 Jan 2026 -- The anticipated announcement of an $800-billion Ukraine reconstruction package has been sidelined amid disagreements between the United States and Europe over Greenland and the US-proposed Board of Peace for Gaza, according to media reports, citing officials.
  • Ranking Member Shaheen Pushes for Congress to Prohibit Taking Greenland by Passing the NATO Unity Protection Act US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 21 Jan 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senator Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, released a statement after President Trump's repeated threats to acquire Greenland during his speech at the World Economic Forum in Davos.
  • France Pushes for NATO Drills in Greenland, Pledges to Join - Reports Sputnik 21 Jan 2026 -- France has called for NATO exercises to be held in Greenland and has expressed its readiness to join the drills, according to media reports Wednesday, citing the French president's office.

  • Chairman Mast, HFAC, advances AI Overwatch Act U.S. House of Representatives Committee on Foreign Affairs 21 Jan 2026 -- Today, the House Foreign Affairs Committee advanced the AI Overwatch Act as part of a full committee markup.
  • Trump says he will skip G7 emergency summit, questions NATO's value to U.S. People's Daily 21 Jan 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Tuesday he will not attend an emergency Group of Seven summit proposed by French President Emmanuel Macron.
  • Space Forces Southern activated at Davis-Monthan AFB SOUTHCOM 21 Jan 2026 -- The U.S. Space Force and U.S. Southern Command officially marked the activation of Space Forces Southern (SPACEFOR-SOUTH) during a ceremony at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base, Jan. 21, 2026, formalizing the command's role in integrating space capabilities across the Western Hemisphere.
  • 29th ID Participates in Warfighter Exercise in Finland Army News 21 Jan 2026 -- Virginia National Guard Soldiers assigned to the Fort Belvoir-based 29th Infantry Division Headquarters participated in the Finnish training exercise Operation Karelian Spear Nov. 28 - Dec. 4, 2025.

  • Remarks by NATO Secretary General at World Economic Forum, Davos NATO 21 Jan 2026
  • Pentagon to Scale Back US Participation in Several NATO Advisory Groups - Reports Sputnik 21 Jan 2026 -- The US Department of War plans to cut back US participation in several NATO advisory groups, The Washington Post newspaper reported, citing sources.

  • Russia-Myanmar Working Group on Military Cooperation holds meeting at Ministry of Defence of Russian Federation Russia MoD 21 Jan 2026 -- A regular meeting of the Russia-Myanmar Working Group on Military Cooperation took place at the Ministry of Defence of the Russian Federation on 21 January 2026.

  • Ministry of Defence launches Brave1 Dataroom, a secure environment for training military AI solutions Ukraine MOD 21 Jan 2026
  • Ukraine and the United Kingdom are already implementing joint defence manufacturing projects under the One Hundred Year Partnership Ukraine MOD 21 Jan 2026
  • Oleksandr Kozenko discusses defence partnership development with the Undersecretary of the Ministry of Defence of the United Arab Emirates Ukraine MOD 21 Jan 2026

  • U.S. House Committee approves US$1.15 billion for Taiwan defense CNA 21 Jan 2026 -- A U.S. House of Representative committee released its version of the 2026 Consolidated Appropriations Act on Monday that included US$1.15 billion (NT$36.4 billion) to support security cooperation with Taiwan.

  • Training academy boosts North Queensland aviation workforce Australia DOD 21 Jan 2026 -- North Queensland's future Apache Helicopters maintenance workforce has commenced training this week following the opening of the Townsville Aviation Training Academy.

  • High North security set to be focus of Defence Secretary visit to Denmark UK MOD 21 Jan 2026 -- Defence talks in Copenhagen today will bring the UK and Denmark together to discuss High North security, industrial cooperation, and vital support to Ukraine, with Defence Secretary John Healey MP meeting Danish Defence Minister Troels Lund Poulsen.

  • Trump says Iron Dome is US technology, not Israel's Press TV 21 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has said that the Iron Dome anti-rocket system, widely deployed by the Israeli regime in regional wars, was developed by the United States, and Tel Aviv should not portray it as an Israeli technology.
  • Israeli military chief warns Netanyahu, Katz of manpower crisis undermining military readiness Press TV 21 Jan 2026 -- The Israeli military's chief of staff has warned prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu and minister of military affairs Israel Katz that a severe manpower shortage is undermining the regime's military preparedness.
  • The Chief of the General Staff to Ultra-Orthodox Troops Israel Defense Forces 21 Jan 2026
  • The Chief of the General Staff's Remarks to Pilots at Nevatim Air Base Israel Defense Forces 21 Jan 2026

  • Cutting-edge drone degree to train military forces of the future unveiled UK MOD 21 Jan 2026 -- Young engineers to master battlefield-proven technology at Hereford university from September 2026.

  • Minister of Defence Troels Lund Poulsen meets Secretary of State for Defence John Healy in Copenhagen Danish Ministry of Defence 21 Jan 2026 -- The Secretary of State for Defence for the United Kingdom, John Healey, will today meet with Minister of Defence Troels Lund Poulsen in Copenhagen to discuss a strengthening the defence cooperation between Denmark and the United Kingdom.

  • Undersecretary Roger after the NB8 political directors' meeting: supporting Ukraine and increasing pressure on Russia to achieve a just and lasting peace is a priority of our Nordic-Baltic chairmanship in 2026 Estonia MFA 21 Jan 2026 -- Yesterday and today, on 20 and 21 January, the Foreign Ministry's Undersecretary for Political Affairs, Martin Roger, hosted the political directors of the foreign ministries of the Nordic and Baltic countries (NB8) in Tallinn.

  • Czech Defence Minister's first visit to Slovakia: Towards the two Nations' strong alliance, cooperation and defence self-sufficiency Czech Republic Ministry of Defence & Armed Forces 21 Jan 2026 -- Today (Wednesday 21 January 2026), we welcomed Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defence of the Czech Republic Jaromír Zůna to Bratislava, in what was his first official visit abroad in his new role.

  • Defence Minister's meeting with the Ambassador of Germany to Bucharest Romania - Ministry of National Defence 21 Jan 2026 -- On Tuesday, January 20th, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, had a meeting with the Ambassador of the Federal Republic of Germany to Romania, H.E. Angela Ganninger, at the Ministry of National Defence headquarters.

  • New guides provide advice on planning and maintenance of civil defence shelters Finnish Government 21 Jan 2026 -- The Ministry of the Interior has published three new guides containing practical instructions on how to use and maintain civil defence shelters and on how to draw up emergency plans. The guides are intended, for example, to support building owners (such as housing companies) and other building operators.

  • A look at the Defence news 12 - 18 January Netherlands Ministry of Defence 21 Jan 2026

  • EU "should not hesitate" to use anti-coercion mechanism over U.S. tariff threats: Macron People's Daily 21 Jan 2026 -- French President Emmanuel Macron said Tuesday that the European Union (EU) "should not hesitate" to deploy its anti-coercion mechanism in response to the U.S. tariff threats linked to Greenland.

  • Prime Minister's Office announcement Israel - Prime Minister 21 Jan 2026 -- "Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has announced that he accepts the invitation of US President Donald Trump and will become a member of the Board of Peace, which is to be comprised of world leaders."

Defense Industry

  • Contracts for Jan. 21, 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 21 January 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 21 Jan 2026
  • UN Assembly president defends multilateralism, UN Charter in Davos UN News 21 Jan 2026 -- From Davos, the President of the UN General Assembly on Wednesday warned that the world has entered a "make-or-break" moment for multilateralism, saying the rules-based order can survive only if states speak the truth and act when it's hard. She called for a cross-regional alliance to push back against growing lawlessness, disinformation, and power-based politics.

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Hamas warns of 'systematic Israeli violations' as Gaza ceasefire teeters Press TV 21 Jan 2026 -- The Palestinian resistance movement Hamas has reaffirmed its commitment to the Gaza ceasefire, warning that Israel's "repeated systematic violations" are undermining the agreement, which has now passed the 100-day mark.
  • Israeli strikes kill 11 across Gaza, including children and journalists: Palestinian medics Press TV 21 Jan 2026 -- Gaza hospital officials report that Israeli forces killed at least 11 Palestinians in separate attacks across the Gaza Strip on Wednesday, including two 13-year-old boys, three journalists, and a woman.

  • Israeli airstrikes kill two in Lebanon despite ceasefire Press TV 21 Jan 2026 -- Israel has conducted new airstrikes on southern Lebanon despite the ceasefire agreement reached in 2024, killing two people.

  • Sanctioning Hamas and Covert Funding Networks US Dept. of State 21 Jan 2026 -- Today, the United States is taking action to expose and disrupt covert Hamas funding networks and their operations. Hamas continues to show a callous disregard for the lives of Gazans.
  • Treasury Exposes and Disrupts Hamas's Covert Support Network US Treasury 21 Jan 2026 -- Today, the Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) is taking action against Hamas's covert links to nonprofit organizations. Hamas's insidious practice of operating behind civilian organizations endangers Palestinians and undermines efforts to build a lasting and prosperous peace. By exposing organizations with secret ties to Hamas, OFAC is strengthening the international community's ability to support legitimate Palestinian interests.

  • Trump says UN should continue, but Board of Peace "might" replace UN People's Daily 21 Jan 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Tuesday that the United Nations should continue because of its potential, but the Board of Peace he proposed "might" replace the organization.

  • President Rumen Radev Received Invitation from the President of the United States Donald Trump to Be Bulgaria's Representative in the Peace Council Initiated by the US President President of the Republic of Bulgaria 21 Jan 2026 -- President Rumen Radev received a letter from US President Donald Trump, inviting Bulgaria to join the founding countries of the Peace Council initiated by the American President, and President Radev to represent our country in the future international organisation.
  • Prime Minister's Office announcement Israel - Prime Minister 21 Jan 2026 -- "Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has announced that he accepts the invitation of US President Donald Trump and will become a member of the Board of Peace, which is to be comprised of world leaders."
  • Netanyahu joins familiar circle of war at heart of Trump's Gaza plan Press TV 21 Jan 2026 -- Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu has "accepted" an invitation from US President Donald Trump to join his self-styled "Board of Peace," a move that has intensified global criticism of a body widely seen as compromised, coercive and deeply entangled in the devastation of Gaza.
  • Most EU Countries Reject US Invitation to Join Board of Peace for Gaza Sputnik 21 Jan 2026 -- Most EU countries have rejected the US invitation to join the Board of Peace for Gaza, media reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with the situation.
  • Joint Statement by the Foreign Ministers of Türkiye, Egypt, Indonesia, Jordan, Pakistan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia and UAE, 21 January 2026 Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 21 Jan 2026
  • No: 14, 21 January 2026, Regarding the Participation of H.E. Hakan Fidan, Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Türkiye, in the Signing Ceremony of the Board of Peace Charter Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 21 Jan 2026
  • Pakistan Accepts Invitation for Joining the Board of Peace (BoP) with the View to Achieving Lasting Peace in Gaza Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 21 Jan 2026 -- In response to the invitation extended to Prime Minister Muhammad Shehbaz Sharif by the President of the United States, H.E. Donald J. Trump, Pakistan would like to announce its decision to join the Board of Peace (BoP) as part of its ongoing efforts to support the implementation of the Gaza Peace Plan under the framework of the United Nations Security Council Resolution 2803.

  • Cold kills another infant in Gaza as West Bank displacement intensifies UN News 21 Jan 2026 -- Another child in the Gaza Strip has died from hypothermia as winter weather continues to whip the enclave, the UN said on Wednesday, citing information from the health authorities.

  • Israel/Palestine: Statement by the Spokesperson on the demolition of the UNRWA compound in East Jerusalem European External Action Service (EEAS) 21 Jan 2026 -- The decision by the Israeli authorities to enter and demolish the UNRWA headquarters in East Jerusalem is a serious attack against the UN agency, and constitutes a violation of Israel's obligations under the Convention on the Privileges and Immunities of the United Nations according to which UN Member States must protect and respect the inviolability of UN premises.
  • OIC Condemns the Demolition of UNRWA Facilities by the Occupation and Calls on the International Community to Shoulder its Responsibilities Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 21 Jan 2026 -- The Organization of Islamic Cooperation has condemned in the strongest terms the Israeli occupation forces' storming and demolition of facilities belonging to the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestinian Refugees (UNRWA) in occupied Jerusalem, considering this a dangerous escalation and a flagrant violation of the Charter of the United Nations and its resolutions, the advisory opinion of the International Court of Justice, and the 1946 Convention on the Privileges and Immunities of the United Nations
  • Spanish Government condemns latest attack on UNRWA facilities in East Jerusalem Spain MFA 21 Jan 2026 -- The Spanish Government condemns the latest attack on UNRWA facilities, carried out this morning in East Jerusalem by Israeli security forces in an unacceptable violation of the privileges and immunities of the United Nations agency.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Mozambique UNOCHA 21 Jan 2026

  • DFA Statement Philippines DFA 21 Jan 2026 -- In response to the remarks made recently by the Chinese Foreign Ministry Spokesman, the DFA affirms its support for publicly elected officials and government institutions performing their lawful duties in defense of Philippine sovereignty, sovereign rights, and jurisdiction.
  • DFA backs PH officials' WPS stance amid Chinese spox 'warning' Philippine News Agency 21 Jan 2026 -- The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) affirmed its position on the West Philippine Sea (WPS) and threw its support behind government and elected officials following the warning issued by China that they would "pay the price" for making supposed provocations.
  • Rajah Sulayman, other OPVs to help secure PH waters, fishers Philippine News Agency 21 Jan 2026 -- The Philippine Navy (PN) said future offshore patrol vessel (OPV) BRP Rajah Sulayman (PS-20) and five other soon-to-arrive sister ships will enhance the country's capability to protect its maritime zones, which include the West Philippine Sea (WPS), and fisherfolk plying its waters.
  • PH, China in talks for 'roadmap' to manage SCS tensions - envoy Philippine News Agency 21 Jan 2026 -- The Philippines and China have reached a "preliminary consensus" for the creation of a roadmap that would help manage tensions in the South China Sea, Chinese Ambassador Jing Quan disclosed Tuesday night.
  • Chinese military expels intruding Philippine aircraft from territorial airspace over Huangyan Dao: spokesperson People's Daily 21 Jan 2026 -- The Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) on Tuesday expelled a Philippine government aircraft that illegally intruded into the territorial airspace over China's Huangyan Dao without the approval of the Chinese government, a spokesperson said.

  • Security Council Press Statement on Terrorist Attack in Afghanistan UN Security Council 21 Jan 2026

  • "We will find the dirty hands that attacked our flag and hold these traitors to account" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 21 Jan 2026 -- Addressing AK Party's parliamentary group meeting, President Erdoğan said: "We will definitely find the dirty hands that attacked our flag and hold these traitors to account. Investigations have already been launched by the Ministry of National Defense and the Ministry of Justice."

  • IOM Delivers Life-Saving Assistance Following Major Fire in Cox's Bazar Refugee Camp IOM 21 Jan 2026 -- The International Organization for Migration (IOM) has launched an emergency response following a large fire in a Rohingya refugee camp in Cox's Bazar early Tuesday morning. Relief efforts are being carried out in close coordination with the Government of Bangladesh and humanitarian partners to ensure timely and appropriate assistance to affected families.

  • Senators Shaheen, Booker Seek Information on Sanctioned RSF Commander's Presence in the United States US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 21 Jan 2026 -- Yesterday, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, and Cory Booker (D-NJ) sent a letter to U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi, Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent requesting information about the conditions under which a sanctioned senior commander of Sudan's Rapid Support Forces (RSF) was present in Washington, D.C. in October 2025.

  • Joint Statement Following the Positive Agenda Joint Action Plan Meeting Between the Deputy Foreign Ministers of the Republic of Türkiye and the Hellenic Republic, 21 January 2026 Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 21 Jan 2026

  • 'No Time to Waste', Security Council Speakers Stress, as Gangs, Arm Flows Threaten Electoral Progress in Haiti UN Security Council 21 Jan 2026-- While the new year carries "high expectations" for progress in Haiti, the Security Council heard today that achieving this requires sustained security efforts, focused international engagement and assumed political responsibilities.
  • Haiti crisis at breaking point as gangs tighten grip ahead of transition deadline UN News 21 Jan 2026 -- Haiti's deepening crisis has reached a critical phase, senior UN officials warned the Security Council on Wednesday, as powerful gangs continue to expand their control across the country.
  • Haiti explained: why the crisis is deepening — and what comes next UN News 21 Jan 2026 -- Haiti is entering 2026 facing one of the most complex crises in its recent history. On Wednesday, the Caribbean island nation will be high on the international agenda as the UN Security Council holds its first meeting of the year to update ambassadors.
  • The United Kingdom remains deeply concerned by the expansion and intensification of violence in Haiti: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 21 Jan 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador Archie Young, UK Deputy Permanent Representative to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Haiti.

  • At the edge of war: the Central African Republic's uneasy border with Sudan UN News 21 Jan 2026 -- The land flattens on the approach to Birao, a cut-off town ringed by savannah in the far north of the Central African Republic, where roads dissolve into dust and motorcycles vastly outnumber cars. Less than two hours' drive from the Sudanese border, this is the edge of a fractured country still piecing itself back together, while absorbing the shockwaves of a neighbouring conflict.

News Reports

  • 37 Mexican Nationals Wanted for Serious Crimes Transferred to the United States from Mexico, Including Leaders of Foreign Terrorist Organizations US Dept. of Justice 21 Jan 2026 -- The United States announced today that yesterday evening it took into custody 37 fugitives from Mexico facing a range of federal criminal charges around the country, including charges relating to narcoterrorism, providing material support to a foreign terrorist organization, firearms trafficking, human smuggling, money laundering, and various drug trafficking offenses, including conspiracy to traffic methamphetamine, fentanyl, and cocaine.

  • Meeting with permanent members of the Security Council President of Russia 21 Jan 2026 -- Vladimir Putin chaired, via videoconference, a briefing session with permanent members of the Security Council.
  • Meeting with Government members President of Russia 21 Jan 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held a videoconference meeting with Government members. The agenda included issues raised by citizens during the Results of the Year with Vladimir Putin special programme.
  • Statement of the Permanent Representative of the Russian Federation to the United Nations Office and Other International Organizations in Geneva Ambassador Gennady GATILOV at the plenary meeting of the Conference on Disarmament, Geneva, January 20, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 21 Jan 2026
  • Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova's answer to a media question regarding Moldova's withdrawal from the Commonwealth of Independent States Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 21 Jan 2026
  • Tsahkna: the EU oil ban now in force increases pressure on Russia Estonia MFA 21 Jan 2026 -- Today, the ban introduced under the European Union's 18th sanctions package entered into force, prohibiting the import into the EU of petroleum products produced in third countries if they have been made using crude oil originating from Russia.
  • Russia Dusts Off Ukrainian-Designed, Soviet-Era Passenger Jets As Sanctions Bite RFE/RL 21 Jan 2026 -- Russia is putting mothballed planes back into service amid sanctions that have limited the country's ability to sustain its passenger fleet. Planes being dusted off include a Ukrainian-designed aircraft that had been grounded over safety concerns.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on January 21, 2026 PRC MofA 21 Jan 2026
  • China expresses support for UN after Trump says Board of Peace "might" replace it People's Daily 21 Jan 2026 -- China has always been committed to true multilateralism, and will firmly safeguard the international system with the United Nations at its core, foreign ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Wednesday.
  • China firmly upholds UN-centered intl system, says Chinese FM in response to Trump's claim that 'Board of Peace' may replace UN Global Times 21 Jan 2026 -- Regarding the "Board of Peace," the Chinese side has already addressed relevant questions yesterday. As for the UN, China has always practiced true multilateralism. No matter how the international situation changes, China firmly upholds the UN-centered international system, the international order based on international law, and the basic norms of international relations underpinned by the purposes and principles of the UN Charter, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Wednesday.
  • China's Foreign Ministry responds to US Trade Rep's remarks on potential new trade talks Global Times 21 Jan 2026 -- Following US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer's remarks at the World Economic Forum in Davos that the US hopes to engage in a new round of potential trade talks, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said at a press conference on Wednesday that "I'd refer you to competent authorities for anything specific" when asked whether China and the US are considering holding such a meeting, and where it would take place.
  • FM addresses query on China's view of Western 'chaos' linked to the Trump administration Global Times 21 Jan 2026 -- When asked whether China welcomes the "chaos" said to have been caused by the Trump administration in the West and the Chinese side's view on the matter as in one way, some observers think China might, to some extent, welcome such "chaos" and in another sense, obviously it is also harming global trade, Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said that China consistently adheres to an independent foreign policy of peace. On the basis of mutual respect and equality, we engage in friendly exchanges with all countries and are always committed to being a positive, stabilizing and constructive force. We have no intention, nor will we, compete with any country for influence.
  • Asked to give advice to European countries on how to deal with US tariff threat over Greenland, Chinese FM responds Global Times 21 Jan 2026 -- When asked by a foreign media journalist to give advice to European countries on how to deal with US tariff threat over Greenland, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Wednesday that regarding relations between European countries and the US, China has no comment, and that on the Greenland issue, China has stated its position on multiple occasions.
  • China will work with relevant countries to jointly safeguard intl peace, tranquility, says FM on inquiry over Asian security model Global Times 21 Jan 2026 -- In a world marked by intertwined turmoil, Asia is widely recognized as a high ground of global development and prosperity. This favorable situation has not come easily and deserves to be cherished, Guo Jiakun, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson said on Wednesday.
  • Supporting diplomatic premises is intl obligation of host countries, FM on UK's approval of plan for China's new embassy Global Times 21 Jan 2026 -- In response to media inquiries on the UK government's approval of China's new embassy plan in London, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said at a press briefing on Wednesday that providing support and convenience for the construction of diplomatic premises is an international obligation of host countries.
  • Supporting diplomatic missions is a host country's international obligation, FM says after UK approves Chinese embassy plan Global Times 21 Jan 2026 -- In response to media inquiry over the UK government's approval of China's new embassy plan in London, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said at a press briefing on Wednesday that providing support and facilities for the construction of diplomatic missions is an international obligation of the host country under international law.

  • U.S. Senate committee to review 4 Taiwan-related bills next week CNA 21 Jan 2026 -- The United States Senate Committee on Foreign Relations will review four Taiwan-related bills on Jan. 29 covering "sovereignty symbols," energy security, undersea cable protection and support for Taiwan's partners.
  • Opposition lawmakers chide Lai at impeachment hearing CNA 21 Jan 2026 -- Opposition lawmakers on Wednesday lashed out at President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) in the Legislature after Lai declined to appear at a hearing on an impeachment motion directed at him.
  • Taiwan revokes residency of Chinese spouse influencer over 'national security' CNA 21 Jan 2026 -- Taiwan's National Immigration Agency (NIA) has revoked the long-term residency permit of a Chinese spouse known online as "Guan Guan" (關關) over "controversial remarks" deemed to pose a potential threat to national security and social stability.
  • Mainland reiterates 1992 Consensus as foundation for resuming cross-Strait dialogue People's Daily 21 Jan 2026 -- Only by recognizing the 1992 Consensus, which embodies the one-China principle, can the two sides of the Taiwan Strait resume dialogue and consultation mechanisms, a Chinese mainland spokesperson said Wednesday.
  • Mainland deals with almost 4,000 cyber attacks from Taiwan in 2025 People's Daily 21 Jan 2026 -- Chinese mainland authorities investigated and handled nearly 4,000 cyber attacks originating from Taiwan last year, up 25 percent year on year, a spokesperson said on Wednesday.
  • Mainland says DPP's so-called trade deal with U.S. sells out Taiwan's interests People's Daily 21 Jan 2026 -- The so-called "consensus" reached by Taiwan's Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) authorities in tariff talks with the United States amounts to a betrayal of the well-being of Taiwan people and the interests of its industrial development, a Chinese mainland spokesperson said on Wednesday.
  • Strengthening trade bonds benefits people across Taiwan Strait: spokesperson People's Daily 21 Jan 2026 -- A Chinese mainland spokesperson said Wednesday that strengthening economic cooperation and advancing integrated development serve the common interests of compatriots across the Taiwan Strait.
  • Mainland says DPP's so-called trade deal with U.S. sells out Taiwan's interests People's Daily 21 Jan 2026 -- The so-called "consensus" reached by Taiwan's Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) authorities in tariff talks with the United States amounts to a betrayal of the well-being of Taiwan people and the interests of its industrial development, a Chinese mainland spokesperson said on Wednesday.
  • U.S. naval ships transit Taiwan Strait for first time in 2026 CNA 21 Jan 2026 -- Two U.S. naval vessels transited the Taiwan Strait last Friday and Saturday, marking the first such passage by American naval ships this year, according to a USNI News report.
  • So-called trade deal between US and Taiwan region would turn 'tech island' into 'hollow island': mainland spokesperson Global Times 21 Jan 2026 -- The so-called "trade deal" reached between the US and Taiwan region is, in essence, a collusion between external forces and "Taiwan independence" separatist forces. It is a "sell-out contract" that plunders the hard-earned money of the Taiwan residents and hollows out the foundations of Taiwan's industries, harming the future of Taiwan residents and the region's industrial development, Peng Qing'en, a spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, said on Wednesday.
  • DPP authorities' arms purchases squander money of people to amass own wealth: mainland spokesperson Global Times 21 Jan 2026 -- Taiwan's defense authorities have recently been mired in sustained public controversy over multiple arms procurements. A spokesperson for the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council stated that, in order to seek "independence" through military means, the Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) authorities are wantonly squandering the hard-earned money of Taiwan residents to purchase foreign weapons, while taking the opportunity to amass wealth and engage in corruption.
  • China opposes any form of official exchanges between countries that have diplomatic relations with China and the Taiwan region: mainland spokesperson Global Times 21 Jan 2026 -- China firmly opposes any form of official exchanges between countries that have diplomatic relations with China and the Taiwan region. The one-China principle is a basic norm governing international relations and a widely recognized consensus of the international community, Peng Qing'en, a spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, said on Wednesday at a regular press conference.
  • PLA Southern Theater Command's drone flight training entirely legitimate, lawful: mainland spokesperson slams DPP authorities' claims Global Times 21 Jan 2026 -- The PLA Southern Theater Command organized routine drone flight training in the airspace near China's Dongsha Island, which is entirely legitimate and lawful, Peng Qing'en, a spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, said at a regular press conference on Wednesday, when asked to comment on claims by Taiwan's defense authorities that a mainland drone had entered airspace within 12 nautical miles of China's Dongsha Island and that the move was a so-called "highly provocative and irresponsible act" that undermined regional peace and stability and violated international law.
  • DPP's so-called cyber intrusions report attempts to distort facts; 4,000 cyberattack incidents from Taiwan direction handled in 2025: mainland spokesperson Global Times 21 Jan 2026 -- When asked to comment on a so-called report released by the DPP authorities recently, which claims that in 2025 the mainland carried out "comprehensive" cyber intrusions against critical infrastructure on the Taiwan island, Peng Qing'en, a spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, said on Wednesday that the DPP authorities are fabricating rumors and engaging in slander and smears against the mainland, even at the cost of escalating cross-Straits confrontation, in order to cover up their own governance failures, divert public dissatisfaction, and pursue political self-interest.
  • 'Sellout contract, 'letter of surrender': Mainland spokesperson slams DPP authorities' claim that tariff talks with US reached preset goals Global Times 21 Jan 2026 -- Peng Qing'en, spokesperson for the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, on Wednesday slammed the Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) authorities claim that tariff talks with US reached preset goals, saying the so-called consensus is a "sellout contract" and a "letter surrender."
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 21 Jan 2026
  • Mainland spokesperson slams DPP authorities for hyping so-called 'mainland threat' narrative over man in Taiwan accused of spying Global Times 21 Jan 2026 -- Peng Qing'en, a spokesperson for the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, said on Wednesday that the Lai Ching-te authorities have repeatedly exaggerated the so-called "mainland threat," escalating cross-Straits confrontation and antagonism. He noted that such actions run counter to the mainstream public opinion on the island, which calls for peace, development, exchanges, and cooperation across the Taiwan Straits, are unpopular, and are bound to face opposition.

  • President Lee, Qatar cover defense, energy in 2nd phone talks KOREA.net 21 Jan 2026 -- President Lee Jae Myung on Jan. 20 at Cheong Wa Dae in Seoul held phone talks with Qatari Emir Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani.

  • 'Think differently': Araghchi urges Trump to change tack after trying 'every conceivable hostile act' Press TV 21 Jan 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has demanded a fundamental shift in US policy following a year of failed aggression and foreign-backed destabilization efforts targeting Iran.
  • US 'must be held accountable' for interference, support for terrorism in Iran: Araghchi Press TV 21 Jan 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has called for holding the United States legally accountable for blatant interference in Iran's internal affairs, denouncing Washington for backing organized terrorist violence aimed at destabilizing the country following recent riots.
  • US Blames Iranian Regime As Death Toll From Brutal Crackdown On Protesters Rises RFE/RL 21 Jan 2026 -- The United States says recent protests across Iran were the result of the regime's "mismanagement" and not foreign influences, as claimed by Tehran, as the death toll continues to rise from a violent crackdown on the biggest threat to the Islamic republic in years.
  • US Treasury Secretary admits sanctions seek to destabilize Iran's economy, fuel unrest Press TV 21 Jan 2026 -- US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has openly acknowledged the role of US sanctions in destabilizing Iran's economy and fueling public unrest.
  • Witkoff: US Not Currently Negotiating With Iran RFE/RL 21 Jan 2026 -- US special envoy Steve Witkoff, speaking on behalf of the Trump administration, said the US isn't negotiating with Iran now, although "we've had contact with them."
  • 'Bodies Behind Every Car': Witnesses Recount No-Mercy Killings In Iran's Central Cities RFE/RL 21 Jan 2026 -- In the industrial and residential suburbs of Iran's central Isfahan Province, three eyewitnesses have described scenes of extraordinary violence during the recent wave of anti-regime protests.
  • Iran dismantles terrorist cell: Ringleader killed, 11 members arrested Press TV 21 Jan 2026 -- Iranian security and intelligence forces have killed a ringleader and nearly a dozen members of a Takfiri terrorist cell in a series of coordinated operations in the southeastern province of Sistan and Baluchestan, officials say.

  • Press conference - Parliament House, Canberra Prime Minister of Australia 21 Jan 2026

  • Prime Minister Carney meets with Secretary General of NATO Mark Rutte Prime Minister of Canada 21 Jan 2026 -- Today, the Prime Minister, Mark Carney, met with the Secretary General of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO), Mark Rutte, on the margins of the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting in Davos, Switzerland.
  • Prime Minister Carney meets with Prime Minister of Sweden Ulf Kristersson Prime Minister of Canada 21 Jan 2026 -- Today, the Prime Minister, Mark Carney, met with the Prime Minister of Sweden, Ulf Kristersson, on the margins of the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting in Davos, Switzerland.
  • Canada PM: World order in 'midst of a rupture' from American hegemony Press TV 21 Jan 2026 -- Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney warns that the so-called US-led global system of governance is breaking apart, driven by the expansionist posture and raw power politics of President Donald Trump's administration.

  • Poland and Latvia continue strategic cooperation for security in the region Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 21 Jan 2026 -- On Wednesday, 21 January, deputy prime minister - minister of national defence, Władysław Kosiniak-Kamysz, has met Andris Sprūds, the minister of defence of Latvia, in Warsaw.

  • Speech by Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul at the German Embassy in Nairobi Germany Federal Foreign Office 21 Jan 2026

  • Speech by President von der Leyen at the EP Plenary debate on the conclusions of the European Council meeting of 18 December 2025 and a stronger and more sovereign Europe amid rising geopolitical tensions and continuous threats to the rules-based order European Commission 21 Jan 2026

  • Labor Disputes, Security Fears Test China's Economic Projects In Tajikistan RFE/RL 21 Jan 2026 -- After years of deepening engagement through mining operations, road construction, and industrial projects, Chinese companies operating in Tajikistan now face challenges from the communities that host their investments.
